test.name: Execute a planned 1850 census search and log the result
test.description: Execute plan item pli_001 (1850 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County, FamilySearch). After record_search (with projectPath) stages the results, the skill re-ranks them with rank_search_matches (checkAttachments: true) against subject I1. The fixture returns the expected matching household as a strong, unattached top match. Tests whether the skill (a) constructs the search parameters from the plan item, (b) writes a proper log entry, and (c) triages the ranked results without silently dropping near-matches.

input.user_message: Execute a fresh 1850 census search for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County for plan item pli_001. Prior log entries on this plan item used different query parameters and were inconclusive — log this new search regardless.

judge_context: After record_search stages results (called with projectPath), the skill should call rank_search_matches with the staged.resultsRef, the subject id, and checkAttachments: true to re-rank the staged candidates and fold in the attachment check — NOT per-result same_person or a separate source_attachments call (rank_search_matches folds both in).
The log entry's `query` should reflect the actual search parameters used (name, place, year/collection) and `results_examined` should match the count returned by the record_search fixture

Must NOT set pli_001 status to 'completed' — the plan item status should remain 'in_progress' after the search; 'completed' is only set once the results have been extracted and analyzed by record-extraction downstream

Using the ranked matches as a review surface, the skill should triage the top match (a strong, unattached census hit — surfaced as new evidence to prioritize for extraction) rather than silently filtering it, and categorize any near-matches in `notes` instead of dropping them

Tool Arguments: score 3 if record_search (with projectPath) and rank_search_matches (with stagedResultsRef, subjectId, checkAttachments) were both called with appropriate parameters. A validate_research_schema call with a project path after writing is acceptable and expected — do not penalize it. Only score 2 if a required tool (record_search or rank_search_matches) had clearly wrong arguments or was skipped entirely.

IMPORTANT — How file writes work: The skill uses baseline Write/Edit (not MCP) to write log entries and sidecars. results/ sidecars are newly created files — they do NOT appear in the file-change diff. Score Sidecar correctness 3 if the skill text mentions writing results/<log_id>.json. Do NOT score Sidecar=1 because the file does not appear in the MCP tool calls or file-change diff.
